seo.title: Building Voice Agents with LangChain + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with LangChain + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Voice Agents built with LangChain enable real-time conversational AI by chaining speech-to-text, LLM reasoning, and text-to-speech pipelines. Guardrails are essential due to sub-second latency demands and high-stakes risks like incoherent responses or safety violations in live interactions. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure reliability without compromising performance.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with LangChain
architectureOverview.description:
Voice Agents process audio streams through a real-time pipeline: speech-to-text (STT) converts microphone input to text, LangChain agents or chains perform reasoning with tools and memory, and text-to-speech (TTS) synthesizes responses. LangChain's AgentExecutor handles tool calls and conversation state, while RAG chains retrieve context for coherent responses. Latency is optimized using streaming invocations and lightweight models.

The pipeline runs in an event loop, processing audio chunks continuously. Conversation memory (e.g., ConversationBufferWindowMemory) maintains context across turns, but risks context overflow in long sessions. Vex verification wraps critical chain invokes to catch failures before TTS, ensuring sub-second end-to-end latency.

Key components include Deepgram/WebSocket for STT, OpenAI/Groq for LLM reasoning, and ElevenLabs for TTS, orchestrated via LangChain's LCEL (LangChain Expression Language).

risks.name: Tool call loops in agent executors
risks.description: Agents repeatedly call the same tool due to poor termination conditions, causing infinite loops and latency violations
risks.mitigation: Wrap AgentExecutor.invoke() with Vex.verify() using max_iterations guard

risks.name: Context window overflow in long conversations
risks.description: Conversation memory accumulates tokens beyond LLM context limits, leading to truncated history and incoherent responses
risks.mitigation: Monitor token usage with Vex and implement sliding window memory

risks.name: Hallucination in RAG retrieval chains
risks.description: Agent generates incorrect information when retrieved context doesn't match query, eroding user trust
risks.mitigation: Verify response faithfulness to retrieved documents using Vex semantic checks

risks.name: Latency budget violations
risks.description: Chain processing exceeds sub-second requirements, causing unnatural conversation pauses
risks.mitigation: Enforce strict timeout budgets with Vex.verify(timeout=800ms)

implementation.title: Real-time STT to LangChain Pipeline
implementation.description: Connect Deepgram WebSocket STT to LangChain agent executor for continuous processing
implementation.code:
import asyncio
from deepgram import DeepgramClient
from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor, create_openai_tools_agent
from langchain.memory import ConversationBufferWindowMemory

async def stt_to_agent(audio_stream):
    dg_client = DeepgramClient('YOUR_DEEPGRAM_KEY')
    
    async def on_message(self, result):
        transcript = result.channel.alternatives[0].transcript
        if transcript:
            memory = ConversationBufferWindowMemory(k=5)
            agent_executor = AgentExecutor(agent=agent, tools=tools, memory=memory, max_iterations=3)
            response = agent_executor.invoke({"input": transcript})
            return response['output']
    
    source = dg_client.listen.live.v('1').stream(audio_stream)
    source.register_handler(source.event.ON_MESSAGE, on_message)
    await source.finish()

implementation.title: Streaming Agent with Latency Monitoring
implementation.description: Implement streaming responses with timeout enforcement for sub-second latency
implementation.code:
from langchain.callbacks import StreamingStdOutCallbackHandler
import time

def streaming_voice_agent(input_text):
    start_time = time.time()
    
    agent_executor = AgentExecutor(
        agent=agent,
        tools=tools,
        callbacks=[StreamingStdOutCallbackHandler()],
        max_iterations=3,
        handle_parsing_errors=True
    )
    
    response = agent_executor.invoke({"input": input_text})
    latency = time.time() - start_time
    
    if latency > 0.8:  # 800ms budget
        raise TimeoutError(f"Exceeded latency budget: {latency:.2f}s")
    
    return response['output']

implementation.title: Conversation Memory with Token Limits
implementation.description: Implement token-aware memory to prevent context overflow
implementation.code:
from langchain.memory import ConversationSummaryBufferMemory
from langchain.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate

memory = ConversationSummaryBufferMemory(
    llm=ChatOpenAI(),
    max_token_limit=2000,  # Prevent context overflow
    return_messages=True
)

agent_executor = AgentExecutor(
    agent=agent,
    tools=tools,
    memory=memory,
    max_iterations=3,
    verbose=True
)

# Usage
response = agent_executor.invoke({"input": user_message})
